description: Skill resolution and parse-failure parity (Algorithm A-SKILL). Locks the 2026-06-02 decision: missing/invalid skillId and unparseable parts yield a FAILED task (a Task exists), NOT a JSON-RPC -32602. The runner drives the server/executor with `params` (a message/send payload), collects the emitted events, and asserts the task reaches TASK_STATE_FAILED with the given status message. A genuinely missing `message` envelope is protocol-level (no task) and stays a JSON-RPC error (error_cases, dispatch layer).

test_cases.description: message has parts but no metadata.skillId -> FAILED task, not -32602.

TextPart with invalid JSON for a module whose input schema is object-typed -> FAILED task (the converter-level error surfaces as a failed Task once a Task exists). Requires the target skill to be registered with an object input schema.

error_cases.description: No `message` key at all -> protocol-level JSON-RPC error, no task created. This is dispatch-layer; runners that drive the executor directly (rather than the JSON-RPC server) skip this case and rely on per-SDK server tests.

known_divergences: Event emission lives in the executor (Python/TS: ApCoreAgentExecutor) but in the handler for Rust (handlers.rs); both produce the same observable A2A events. The initial event is a `task` carrying TASK_STATE_SUBMITTED, followed by a `statusUpdate` with TASK_STATE_FAILED — there is no separate SUBMITTED statusUpdate event.

Failed-status messages for missing/unknown skill are exact and cross-language ('Missing required parameter: metadata.skillId', 'Skill not found: <id>'). The unparseable-text-part FAILED message is SDK-specific and therefore NOT asserted here: Python/TS surface the converter message 'TextPart text is not valid JSON' (locked at the converter level in part_conversion.json), while Rust passes the raw text to the module and surfaces the sanitized 'Internal server error'. The cross-language invariant asserted for that case is only that the task FAILS.
Messages live at status.message.parts[0].text (a text Part). The request message role is the wire enum 'ROLE_USER' and skillId lives in params.metadata (Rust also accepts message.metadata).
